stories.title: Cavaliers Survive OT Thriller, Lead Pistons 3-2

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The Cavs trailed 103-94 with 3 minutes left.

Then James Harden took over.

Cleveland wins 117-113 in OT. Harden: 30 pts, 11-14 FT, 8 reb, 6 ast. Cavs lead the series 3-2. #LetEmKnow

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Ausar Thompson had the ball. Clock expired. No foul called.

Detroit wants answers — and the whole city is furious.

Cavs 117, Pistons 113. Overtime. Refs at center of the storm. #DetroitBasketball #LetEmKnow

facebook.long_form:
Nobody told the Cavaliers they were supposed to lose.

Cleveland trailed the Detroit Pistons 103-94 with under three minutes left in regulation on Wednesday night. In any other game, that's a wrap. Not this time.

James Harden — at 36 years old — orchestrated one of the gutsiest clutch stretches of his career. He finished with 30 points on 11-of-14 free throws, grabbed 8 rebounds, and dished 6 assists. Donovan Mitchell added 21. Evan Mobley made a pair of free throws to tie the game at 103 with 45.2 seconds left.

But the biggest moment might have been the non-call. As regulation expired, Detroit's Ausar Thompson appeared to have the basketball ripped from his grasp — and then be tripped by Jarrett Allen — as the clock hit zero. The referees let it go. Detroit erupted. Cade Cunningham immediately challenged the decision postgame.

Cleveland went on a 13-0 run from late in regulation through overtime and won 117-113. The Cavaliers lead this series 3-2 and are one win from their first Eastern Conference Finals appearance since 2018.

Game 6 is Friday at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse in Cleveland.

Was the no-call the right call? Drop your take in the comments.
facebook.image_caption: Cavs rally from 9 down, win 117-113 in OT. Harden clutch again. Series 3-2 Cleveland. Game 6 Friday. #LetEmKnow

article.headline: Cavaliers Survive OT Thriller 117-113, Harden's Clutch 30 Points Lead Comeback Over Pistons

stories.title: Wemby Erupts for 27/17/5 — Spurs Rout Wolves 126-97, Lead 3-2

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
One game after getting ejected — Wemby came back with 27 pts, 17 reb, 5 ast, 3 blk.

He scored 18 of those 27 in the FIRST QUARTER.

Spurs 126, Wolves 97. San Antonio leads 3-2. Game 6 Friday. #Spurs #Timberwolves

facebook.long_form:
Victor Wembanyama does not need a grace period.

One game after being ejected for a Flagrant 2 foul, the 22-year-old Spurs superstar came out on Tuesday and put up one of the most dominant playoff performances this postseason: 27 points, 17 rebounds, 5 assists, and 3 blocks.

He scored 18 of his 27 points in the first quarter alone.

The Timberwolves had no answers. San Antonio cruised to a 126-97 victory and now leads the Western Conference semifinal series 3-2. Game 6 is Friday night at Target Center in Minneapolis at 9:30 PM ET on Prime Video.

If the Spurs win, they advance to the Western Conference Finals to face the Oklahoma City Thunder — a team that is a perfect 8-0 in the 2026 playoffs.

Wemby's response game was as emphatic as it gets. The entire basketball world took notice.

Can anyone stop this kid? Let us know below.
facebook.image_caption: Wemby: 18 pts in Q1. 27/17/5/3 total. Spurs 126, Wolves 97. San Antonio one win from the WCF. #Spurs

article.headline: Wemby Explodes for 27/17/5/3, Spurs Rout Timberwolves 126-97 to Lead Series 3-2

stories.title: Cade Cunningham's 39-Point War Wasn't Enough — Pistons on the Brink

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Cade Cunningham: 39 points. 9 assists.

Still lost in overtime.

Detroit now faces elimination in Game 6 at Cleveland on Friday. Cade left everything on the floor — it just wasn't enough. #DetroitBasketball

facebook.long_form:
Cade Cunningham had the game of his playoff career — and the Detroit Pistons still lost.

Cunningham scored 39 points and added 9 assists Wednesday night, turning in a performance that any other night would have been enough to win. The Pistons led by 15 in the first half. They led 103-94 with under three minutes left in regulation.

Then James Harden took over. The Cavs went on a 13-0 run. A controversial no-call — with Detroit's Ausar Thompson appearing to get tripped by Jarrett Allen as the clock expired — sent the game to overtime instead of giving Detroit a potential game-winning possession.

In overtime, Cleveland finished it: 117-113 final.

Now Detroit must win Game 6 on the road at Cleveland on Friday, or the season is over.

Cade Cunningham was everything the Pistons needed. The game — and a disputed call — just didn't cooperate.

Can the Pistons dig deep one more time on the road? Sound off below.
facebook.image_caption: Cade: 39 pts, 9 ast. Pistons lose in OT. Detroit faces elimination in Game 6 at Cleveland on Friday. #DetroitBasketball

article.headline: Cade's 39-Point War Goes to Waste: Pistons Face Elimination After Heartbreaking OT Loss

stories.title: Knicks Wait, OKC Rests — Conference Finals Picture Comes Into Focus

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The bracket is almost set.

Knicks (waiting). OKC (8-0, waiting).

Two Game 6s on Friday: Cavs-Pistons at Cleveland + Spurs-Wolves at Minnesota. 

The Conference Finals are almost here. Who's your pick? #NBA

facebook.long_form:
Two teams are watching. The rest of the bracket is almost complete.

The New York Knicks swept the Philadelphia 76ers and have been resting for days. The Oklahoma City Thunder are a perfect 8-0 in the 2026 playoffs after sweeping the Los Angeles Lakers. Both No. 1 seeds are ready and waiting for their next opponent.

Two massive Game 6s on Friday will set the conference finals:

— Cleveland hosts Detroit (Cavs lead 3-2, 7 PM ET)
— Minnesota hosts San Antonio (series tied 3-2, 9:30 PM ET)

If Cleveland wins Friday, the Eastern Conference Finals could begin as early as Sunday, May 17 — Knicks vs. Cavaliers.

If San Antonio wins Friday, the Western Conference Finals begin Monday, May 18 — Thunder vs. Spurs.

Conference finals basketball is almost here. Which matchup are you most excited for?
facebook.image_caption: Knicks waiting. OKC waiting. Two Game 6s Friday set the conference finals. The bracket is almost complete. #NBA

article.headline: Knicks and OKC Are Watching: Two Game 6s Friday Will Complete the Conference Finals Bracket

stories.title: Harden Joins LeBron, Kobe, and MJ in Exclusive Playoff Club

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
LeBron. Kobe. MJ.

And now — James Harden.

At 36, Harden joined three of the greatest ever in an elite NBA playoff club with his 30-pt OT performance Wednesday night. The man does not quit. #LetEmKnow #NBA

facebook.long_form:
It's not a list many people expected James Harden to join.

His 30-point, 8-rebound, 6-assist performance in Wednesday night's overtime victory over the Detroit Pistons placed Harden alongside three of the greatest players in NBA history: LeBron James, Kobe Bryant, and Michael Jordan. The four men share a rare distinction in playoff history tied to production, age, and postseason impact.

Harden is 36. He was supposed to be a complementary piece on this Cleveland team — a veteran facilitator who could hit shots and handle the ball in a pinch. Instead, he's been the clutch architect of the Cavaliers' comeback wins in this series.

He shot 8-for-21 from the field, which won't make the highlight reel. But 11-for-14 at the free throw line when the game was on the line? That's the version of Harden that playoff basketball demands.

Great players find ways. Harden has always found ways. That he's doing it at this age, in this moment, makes it all the more remarkable.

The company is rare. The performance was earned.

Did you expect Harden to still be this good at 36? Drop your thoughts below.
facebook.image_caption: LeBron. Kobe. MJ. And now — James Harden. The man joined some elite company Wednesday night at 36. Still got it. #NBA

article.headline: At 36, James Harden Joins LeBron, Kobe, and MJ in Elite Playoff Club After OT Masterclass
